title: C++ Exception Handling in Class Hierarchies
description: A flowchart demonstrating the order of catch block execution when exceptions are thrown from derived classes, showing how the type-matching process works from most-specific to least-specific.
relationships: Exception thrown in try block flows to catch blocks sequentially
First matching catch block handles the exception
Derived class exceptions can be caught by base class handlers
Ellipsis acts as a catch-all handler if no other matches are found
